description_long: This futuristic airplane features a sleek, angular fuselage with a forward-positioned, tinted canopy cockpit. The wings are mid-mounted and swept back, each supporting large, circular ducted fans or engines with red accents, suggesting vertical takeoff and landing capability. The tail section is distinctive, with twin vertical stabilizers and a split horizontal stabilizer, giving it an aggressive, high-tech appearance. The landing gear is not visible, implying it may be retractable or integrated into the body. The color scheme is primarily light gray with dark gray and red highlights, and the surface appears smooth and metallic. The overall design suggests a high-performance, possibly unmanned or advanced piloted urban aircraft.
description: A sleek, futuristic airplane with swept mid-mounted wings, large ducted engines, a forward tinted cockpit, and twin vertical stabilizers, finished in gray with red accents.
description_view_0: Front view shows the angular nose, forward canopy, and prominent circular engines on each wing.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the swept wings, large engine housings, and the split twin tail design.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the twin vertical stabilizers, split horizontal stabilizer, and the symmetry of the engine placement.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, emphasizing the streamlined fuselage and engine integration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with swept wings and integrated large circular engine nacelles; no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin ducted fan engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Twin vertical stabilizers with a split horizontal stabilizer, forming an H-tail configuration.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable or integrated.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single large tinted canopy.
